slides.title: Ambassador Program
body_lines: Defending the Rule of Law in Arizona
slides.speaker_notes: Welcome everyone. Today, we're here as part of a statewide effort to strengthen civic understanding and public trust. This isn't a debate-it's a conversation about the principles that keep our communities fair, stable, and just.
slides.visual_suggestion: Arizona desert horizon watermark, subtle constitutional icon, strong title in #AB1827 and #16294B.

slides.title: Why We're Here
slides.speaker_notes: We're here because trust in law isn't automatic. It's built-by explaining, modeling, and defending fair process. Our goal: help Arizonans see why these principles matter, especially now.
slides.visual_suggestion: Courthouse or civic gathering with Arizona palette; justice icon in corner.
slides.optional_quote: I do solemnly swear (or affirm) that I will support the constitution and laws of the United States and the State of Arizona.

slides.title: What Is the Rule of Law?
slides.speaker_notes: The Rule of Law means no one is above the law. It's about rules everyone can see, processes everyone can trust, and rights everyone can claim.
slides.visual_suggestion: Simple icons: scales, courthouse, open book; Arizona flag colors.
slides.optional_quote: If we don't protect the Rule of Law, we cannot expect the law to protect us.

slides.title: Why It Matters
slides.speaker_notes: The Rule of Law is what lets us resolve differences without force. It protects our freedoms and keeps power in check-so everyone knows where they stand.
slides.visual_suggestion: People linked to institutions; warm, civic palette.

slides.title: Accountability
body_lines: No person or institution is above the law.
slides.speaker_notes: Accountability is the first pillar. It means everyone-public officials and private citizens alike-must answer to the same rules.
slides.visual_suggestion: Accountability icon (gavel or scales) with bold, clear text.

slides.title: Just Law
body_lines: Laws must be clear, public, stable, and protect rights.
slides.speaker_notes: Just law means laws aren't hidden or shifting. They're understandable, public, and designed to protect everyone's rights.
slides.visual_suggestion: Open book or document icon; gold accent rule.

slides.title: Open Government
body_lines: Legal rules and decisions should be transparent.
slides.speaker_notes: Open government means the public can see how decisions are made. Transparency builds trust and keeps power honest.
slides.visual_suggestion: Document or open-door icon; blue and gold palette.

slides.title: Accessible, Impartial Justice
body_lines: Disputes resolved fairly by independent decision-makers.
slides.speaker_notes: Justice must be accessible to all, not just the powerful. Courts and decision-makers must be fair, ethical, and independent.
slides.visual_suggestion: Balanced scales, courthouse steps, or open courtroom.

slides.title: What Happens When It Weakens
slides.speaker_notes: When the Rule of Law weakens, people lose faith in fairness. Rights become fragile, and power-not process-starts to decide outcomes.
slides.visual_suggestion: Subtle, muted warning: faded courthouse or cracked pillar.

slides.title: Why Lawyers Must Lead
slides.speaker_notes: Lawyers are civic translators. Our oath is to the law and the Constitution. We lead by explaining, modeling, and defending fair process for all.
slides.visual_suggestion: Oath-taking or courthouse detail; justice icon in corner.

slides.title: The Ambassador Role
slides.speaker_notes: Ambassadors don't referee politics. We clarify, stay nonpartisan, and invite everyone to see their role in upholding the law.
slides.visual_suggestion: Speaker silhouette with audience; restrained civic branding.

slides.title: Communicating Effectively
slides.speaker_notes: Define terms early. Use examples people recognize. When things drift, bring it back to fairness, accountability, and equal law.
slides.visual_suggestion: Conversation flow or speech bubble icons; audience-centered.

slides.title: What Citizens Can Do
slides.speaker_notes: You don't need a law degree to support the Rule of Law. Learn the basics, expect fairness, and use Arizona's civic resources.
slides.visual_suggestion: Checklist or path graphic; civic icons.

slides.title: Arizona-Specific Resources
slides.speaker_notes: Here are trusted Arizona organizations for follow-up and learning. These are your partners in civic education and public service.
slides.visual_suggestion: Arizona map, resource cards, flag-inspired palette.
slides.optional_quote: I do solemnly swear (or affirm) that I will support the constitution and laws of the United States and the State of Arizona.

slides.title: Call to Action
slides.speaker_notes: Let's keep this going. Teach others, model respectful dialogue, and connect with these organizations for more ways to serve.
slides.visual_suggestion: Clean close with organization logos and space for live links.

slides.title: Closing / Additional Resources
slides.speaker_notes: Thank you for your time and commitment. For more, see the manual, handouts, or Arizona's civic partners. Let's keep the conversation going.
slides.visual_suggestion: Quiet closing slide with further-reading area; desert watermark.
